steps.title: Identify the logical tension
steps.content:
The passage presents a paradox: marginal notes show Cantares Mexicanos mostly predates the Spanish invasion, yet some poems contain inarguable references to Spanish beliefs and customs.

The word Thus signals that scholars' conclusion must resolve this contradiction.

steps.title: Find the resolution
steps.content:
Choice A resolves the paradox by proposing that post-invasion additions to the collection explain the Spanish references.

B and C fail to account for how such references entered the text (poor language knowledge doesn't produce them, and pre-invasion borrowing wouldn't involve contemporary Spanish customs), while D wrongly dismisses them as coincidence despite the passage calling them inarguable.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
